close_reason: Added multi-writer concurrency note to put() doc
description:
Location: secretx-pkcs11/src/lib.rs:362-378 (doc comment on WritableSecretStore::put).

What is unclear: the "Atomicity" section is detailed and accurate for the single-writer case, but does not address the multi-writer case. If two processes both call put() on the same backend pointing at the same token, the sequence:

  P1: find_objects -> [old]
  P2: find_objects -> [old]
  P1: create_object -> new1
  P2: create_object -> new2
  P1: destroy_object(old) -> ok
  P2: destroy_object(old) -> CKR_OBJECT_HANDLE_INVALID (already gone)

leaves both new1 and new2 alive on the token, in indeterminate order. A subsequent get() may return either. There is no PKCS#11-level transaction to prevent this.

What would make it clear: a "Concurrency" sub-section in the put() doc explicitly stating:

  - Single-writer (one secretx-pkcs11 process per token-label tuple): safe, with the documented behavior.
  - Multi-writer (multiple processes writing to the same label): UNSAFE — produces stable duplicates that future get() calls may return either of. Operators must coordinate writes externally.

If the design intent is to support multi-writer, that is a much bigger feature (locking on the HSM via vendor-specific mechanisms, or a coordinator). This crate is unlikely to implement that, so document the constraint instead.

Cross-references: see crate-secretsx-nde2.3 (find_object indeterminism), .4 (destroy_object visibility).
title: put() doc covers single-writer atomicity but is silent on multi-writer concurrency
